entry.title: Switch to the new mass apps for business partner master data on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition!
entry.content:
Do you still use the deprecated apps for mass load and mass maintenance for business partners on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition? It is now to benefit from the successor apps and prepare your environment before the old apps will be removed. This post provides you with more information and details on how to make the switch.

⚠️Important This activity must be completed before the deprecated apps are no longer available! This is planned for release 2608.

 

Why should I switch?

The new apps are enhanced in several dimensions:

  • Useability is enhanced with a new dialog to start a mass change process that allows you to more flexibly define the selection criteria. Selection criteria can now be reviewed and refined from the top of the page. It is no longer required to specify a scope of the change. Overall, this makes working with the app more intuitive.
  • Using data from a file for a mass change or mass load is now directly possible from the aforementioned dialog.
  • These 2 changes make the Manage Mass Maintenance app the central point to start mass processes. No need for the separate Tile Start Mass Processing.
  • The new version of the app provides additional process attributes, such as due date.
  • Customers with the additional license for SAP Master Data Governance can now display each business partner in the app Manage Business Partner – Central Governance, providing a single view on the complete business partner (see Additional Scope Items below).
  • Finally, the new version offers the most recent user experience and generic features of SAP Fiori, for example to collaborate with other users

What must I do?

There are 3 basic steps for making the switch happen:

  1. First, you should finalize all ongoing mass maintenance processes or delete the process if it is no longer relevant. This is recommended as processes created with the deprecated version of the Manage Mass Maintenance app are no longer visible in the new version of the app.
  2. Activate the feature Successor MDG apps for business partners (MDC_BP_CLOUD_READY).
  3. Remove the deprecated Business Catalogs Mass Load - Business Partner Master (Deprecated) (SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSLOAD_PC) and Mass Maintenance - Business Partner Master (Deprecated) (SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSMAINT_PC). Assign the successor Business Catalog Master Data Governance - Mass Maintenance for Business Partners (SAP_CMD_BC_BPCS_MAINT_PC). This will adjust your role to the updated business role template Master Data Specialist - Business Partner Data (SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_SPECIALIST).
  4. Finalize the procedure by starting the app Export Master Data - Business Partners (Fiori ID F6551A) and export any selection of business partners (at least one).
    Important: This is a mandatory step that completes technical setting to enable all new apps!

In case you need more detailed instructions, here they are:

More Detailed Instructions

How to activate the feature?

Follow the documentation Activate Features to find the correct configuration environment according to your system landscape. Eventually, you will find the item Basic Settings and launch this item.

This leads you to the activity Activate Features. Search for the feature Successor MDG apps for business partners (MDC_BP_CLOUD_READY) and activate it. Note: The feature is not reversible, so please test your processes before activating in your production tenant!

How to change the business role for my users?

Examine the business role that you use for your master data specialists using the app Maintain Business Roles. On tab Business Catalogs you will find the two catalogs Mass Load - Business Partner Master (Deprecated) (SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSLOAD_PC) and Mass Maintenance - Business Partner Master (Deprecated) (SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSMAINT_PC). Note that both of them are marked as deprecated.

Remove these catalogs form the business role. Instead, add the catalog Master Data Governance - Mass Maintenance for Business Partners (SAP_CMD_BC_BPCS_MAINT_PC).

You can also use the function Manage Changes After Upgrade that becomes visible once you switch the business role to edit mode.

If your business role is based on the business role template Master Data Specialist - Business Partner Data (SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_SPECIALIST), you can also use the app Business Role Templates to compare and adjust the role.

How to finalize with the export?

It is important to finalize the switch procedure by performing an arbitrary selection of business partners, at least one. Launch the app Export Master Data - Business Partners (Fiori ID F6551A). For example, in the section Define Scope enter BUT000 in the field Select Tables, then enter a business partner number that will select at least one business partner. Also make sure that not too many are selected to avoid spending too much time. After that press Export and if you are satisfied with selected number of business partners, once more Export. There is no need to download the file. It is only important that the export completes.

Done!

You should now be able to use the new app Manage Mass Maintenance – BP (Fiori ID F7974) and all the other new apps included in the new business catalog.

Additional Scope Items

Customers with the additional license for SAP Master Data Governance can active scope item Central Governance for Business Partner (1N7) for additional capabilities.

entry.title: Business Partner Master Data in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced digital economy, the integrity, security, and accessibility of master data are paramount to operational excellence. High-quality Business Partner data forms the foundation of countless critical processes, from procurement and sales to finance and compliance. I am pleased to share some of the latest innovations within SAP S/4HANA Cloud that are designed to elevate your business partner master data management strategy.

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

To strengthen security and data integrity, we implement header-level authorization checks and proactive duplicate detection to prevent unauthorized access and ensure high-quality, reliable data from the outset. At the same time, onward replication across multisystem landscapes and conversational AI through Joule enable seamless synchronization and intuitive access to information, driving efficiency, transparency, and faster decision-making across the enterprise.


This post will provide an overview of four significant new features: 
1. Enhanced security through header-level authorization checks.
2. Proactive data quality management with an API-based duplicate check.
3. Streamlined data synchronization via onward replication in multisystem landscapes.
4. Efficient data access using our conversational AI, Joule.

1. Enable Business Partner Header-Level Authorization Checks Based on Data Controller and Purpose

Data privacy and compliance are non-negotiable. To provide more granular control over sensitive information, we have introduced the ability to enable authorization checks at the business partner header level based on the combination of a Data Controller and a Purpose.

Feature Scope:
This feature allows for the assignment of specific data controller and purpose combinations directly to a business partner record. Subsequently, the system can enforce authorization checks based on these assignments.

Key Benefits:
The primary advantage is a significant enhancement in access control. With this feature activated, a user must be explicitly authorized for the specific data controller and purpose assigned to a business partner to gain access to that record. This ensures that only relevant personnel can view or process sensitive data, strengthening your data governance and compliance posture in line with regulations like GDPR.

 

Business partner: assignment of purposes along with data controllers

2. Business Partner A2X OData API Operation for Duplicate Check

Maintaining a clean and duplicate-free master data repository is a constant challenge, especially in integrated environments. To address this proactively, we have extended the capabilities of our core Business Partner OData service.

Feature Scope:
A new operation for performing a duplicate check has been added to the `API_BUSINESS_PARTNER` A2X OData service. This operation can be invoked for Business Partners, Customers, and Suppliers.

Key Benefits:
This new functionality empowers external applications to identify potential duplicates before a new master data record is created via the API. By embedding this check into the creation process, organizations can prevent the introduction of redundant data at the source. This is a crucial step toward retaining high master data quality, reducing downstream cleanup efforts, and ensuring process consistency across the enterprise.

Please refer to following Blogs:

  • Using Business Partner OData API (Saranga)
  • Deep Payload for Business Partner OData API (Mani)

Duplicate Check in Business Partner A2X

 

3. Business Partner Integration: Onward Replication in a Multisystem Landscape

For organizations managing complex system landscapes, ensuring data consistency across all systems is a major undertaking. Our new onward replication capability simplifies this process significantly.

Feature Scope:
This feature allows you to trigger the onward replication of incoming business partner data from your SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP system to all other connected systems in your landscape. Activation is managed through a simple self-service configuration user interface (SSCUI).

Key Benefits:
This innovation enables your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system to function as a true master data hub. When a business partner is created or changed, the system automatically distributes these updates to all subscribed downstream systems. This automated synchronization ensures data consistency across the entire landscape, reduces manual integration efforts, and minimizes the risk of data-related process failures.

Onward Replication of Business Partner Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP

 

Community Blog: Onward Replication of Business Partner master data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

4. Joule as Efficient Access to Business Partner, Customer, and Supplier Master Data

In our pursuit of a more intelligent and user-friendly ERP experience, we have integrated our conversational AI, Joule, with master data management.

Feature Scope:
Joule now allows users to efficiently access attributes of Business Partner, Customer, and Supplier master data objects using natural language queries.

Key Benefits:
This feature fundamentally streamlines the process of data retrieval for business users. Instead of navigating through multiple Fiori apps and screens, a user can simply ask Joule a question, such as "Show me the addresses for Business Partner 100345" or "What are the dunning data details for Customer XYZ?". The key benefits include:

- Enhanced Productivity: Immediate access to necessary data saves time and effort, allowing analysts to focus on decision-making rather than data retrieval.
- Reduced UI Clicks: Joule provides extremely quick information about attributes without requiring users to open dedicated applications.
- Intuitive Interaction: Natural language input makes data access more accessible to a wider range of users.
BP, Customer, Supplier Master Data in SAP Joule

Joule can provide details ranging from a business partner overview, roles, and relationships to specific customer sales area data and supplier purchasing organization details.

 


Community Blog; Exploring Business Partner Master Data with SAP Joule, the AI Co-pilot

These four features represent a significant step forward in making master data management more secure, intelligent, and efficient within SAP S/4HANA Cloud. By enhancing security, improving data quality at the point of entry, simplifying landscape-wide synchronization, and accelerating user access, we are empowering our customers to build a more robust and reliable data foundation for their enterprise.

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎 Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition – Maintenance Windows and Major Upgrade Windows in 2026
entry.content:
Major Upgrade Windows

As in 2025, the SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition has 4 Major Upgrade Windows in 2026. These 24 hours of potential system unavailability are used to upgrade the systems to the next major version.

  • February 14th – 15th (Release 2602) | Pre-upgrade option for release 2602
  • May 16th – 17th (Release 2605) | Pre-upgrade option for release 2605
  • August 15th – 16th (Release 2608)
  • November 14th – 15th (Release 2611)

Depending on the data center region, the 24 hours downtime will start/end at:

  • Europe:      SAT   4 am – SUN   4 am UTC
  • Americas: SAT 10 am – SUN 10 am UTC

Weekly Maintenance Windows

Potentially every weekend, the SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition could use a downtime of 4 hours, to apply database updates, Hotfix Collections and other changes.

Depending on the data center region, the 4 hours downtime may start at:

  • Europe:      SAT 9 pm UTC
  • Americas: SUN 5 am UTC

The following events for the release 2602 are planned as Weekly Maintenance Windows in 2026:

  • Hotfix Collection ... for Release 2602 | ... (information will be updated accordingly)
  • Hotfix Collection 3 for Release 2605 (only for systems which belong to the pre-upgrade) | May 15th, 2026
  • Hotfix Collection 4 for Release 2605 | May 29th - 31st, 2026
  • Hotfix Collection 5 for Release 2605 | June 12th - 14th, 2026
  • Hotfix Collection 6 for Release 2605 | June 26th - 28th, 2026

Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS)

Please register to Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS) in SAP for Me to get important information about planned and unplanned downtimes:

  • Please register to Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS) in SAP for Me to get important information about planned and unplanned downtimes:

    • Access to Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS): https://me.sap.com/systemsprovisioning/getNotified
    • Find more information about Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS) in this documentation
    • To find SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition entry select:
      • Subscription Type: Cloud Service
      • Solution Area: SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition

References

  • Maintenance Windows and Major Upgrade Windows for SAP Cloud Services
  • SAP Note 2888562 – Intelligent Enterprise Suite: Harmonized release calendar for SAP Cloud products
  • Service Level Agreement for SAP Cloud Services

entry.title: SAP Data Reconciliation: Moving from Matching Maze to The Autonomous Future
entry.content:
BACKGROUND

There are two types of SAP reconciliation meetings during cutover.

Meeting Type 1 (What we planned):

“Let’s quickly confirm data moved correctly.”

Meeting Type 2 (What actually happens):

  • Finance says revenue looks off.
  • Procurement says suppliers are complaining.
  • Supply chain says inventory is inflated.
  • IT says record counts match.
  • Everyone stares at IT.

Welcome to the beautiful chaos of SAP data reconciliation.

Whether you’re:

  • Splitting a company
  • Running S/4 alongside ECC
  • Sharing data with subsidiaries
  • Migrating to a new instance
  • Or integrating systems after M&A

Data matching in SAP is not just technical data reconciliation. It’s business continuity surgery.

Why SAP Reconciliation Feels So Painful Today with current ETL tools?

Let’s be honest about the current state.

We Reconcile Tables, Not Decisions

We check:

  • Counts
  • Totals
  • Field matches

But business runs on:

  • Commitments
  • Revenue recognition
  • Inventory valuation
  • Working capital

Matching tables ≠ preserving business intent.

SAP Processes Are Chains, Not Records

Every transaction is part of a flow:

  • Forecast → Production → Inventory → Sale → Revenue
  • Contract → Order → Delivery → Billing → Cash
  • Budget → Commitment → Accrual → Payment

Break one link, and financial truth bends quietly.

Reconciliation today doesn’t understand chains. It understands spreadsheets.

Customization Is Everywhere

Every SAP system has:

  • Custom fields
  • Custom pricing logic
  • Custom movement types
  • Custom account determination
  • Custom approval flows

Generic reconciliation tools look at this and say: “Good luck.”

Real Business Reconciliation KPIs (Not Row Counts)

Let’s talk about reconciliation the way your CFO thinks about it.

Procure-to-Pay (P2P): The “Where Did My Money Go?” Flow

Business Flow:

PO Amount → GR Amount → Invoice Amount → Payment Amount

Real Business KPIs:

PO to GR Consumption Ratio

What % of PO value has been physically received?

  • Detects over-delivery or under-delivery
  • Identifies ghost POs
  • Highlights operational leakage
If PO is $1M and GR is $1.4M, someone is shopping.

GR to Invoice Variance %

Are vendors invoicing more than what was received?

  • Detects pricing issues
  • Catches duplicate invoicing
  • Flags freight/tax mismatches

This is not technical reconciliation. This is margin protection.

Order-to-Cash (O2C): The “Where Did My Revenue Go?” Flow

Business Flow:

Sales Order → Delivery → Billing → AR → Cash

Real Business KPIs:

Order Fulfillment Integrity

% of orders fully delivered as priced.

  • Detects partial deliveries not billed
  • Finds pricing condition errors
  • Highlights logistics-billing gaps

Delivery-to-Billing Variance

Does billing reflect delivered quantity and agreed price? This catches:

  • Wrong pricing conditions
  • Missed surcharges
  • Freight miscalculation

Revenue leakage hides here.

Manufacturing: The “Why Is My Warehouse Lying?” Flow

Business Flow:

Material → Production → Inventory → Valuation → COGS

Real Business KPIs:

Inventory Valuation Consistency

Does total inventory value match between systems by:

  • Plant
  • Material group
  • Valuation class

Mismatch here = financial statement distortion.

BOM Integrity Impact

% of production orders where:

  • Component consumption matches BOM
  • Standard cost matches actual cost

Incorrect BOM migration? It is cost explosion.

The Real Question for the Future

When we reshare data between SAP systems in the future, we should not ask:

“Did the data copy and tables and fields match?”

We should ask:

“Did the business outcome stay intact?”

That is a completely different problem. And it requires a completely different capability set.

The Future: Business-First, Agentic SAP Reconciliation

Let’s imagine what reconciliation should look like 5 years from now.

Not reactive. Not 400 dashboards..

But autonomous, business-aware, and proactive.

Flow-Centric Reconciliation

Instead of reconciling:

  • Material master
  • Vendor master
  • GL master

We reconcile:

  • Revenue lifecycle integrity
  • Working capital stability
  • Margin preservation
  • Inventory flow accuracy
  • Commitment-to-cash cycles

Example:

Instead of: “Material record count and fields matches.” The system says:

“Inventory valuation variance of 1.8% driven by missing moving average price adjustments in 3 plants. Financial impact: $2.1M.”

That’s business reconciliation.

Agentic Automation Capabilities of Future Data Reconciliation Tool

Here’s what real agentic SAP reconciliation must be capable of:

1. Business Context Modeling

The system must understand:

  • How revenue is recognized
  • How inventory is valued
  • How pricing conditions affect margin
  • How tax determination impacts compliance
  • How cost flows through production

Not just tables — but intent.

2. Cross-Process Correlation

If:

  • Billing increases
  • But cash conversion drops
  • And AR aging shifts

The system correlates these across modules. SD + FI + CO + Treasury. Today, that takes 3 teams and 2 weeks.

Future system: “Cash conversion delay traced to payment term misalignment for customers migrated from legacy region.”

3. KPI Stability Monitoring

When data is migration between SAP systems, the system should monitor:

  • Revenue per customer
  • Margin per product
  • Inventory turns
  • Working capital ratio
  • DSO / DPO shifts
  • Production yield

If KPIs deviate outside normal behavior bands. Alert triggered. Because business drift matters more than table drift.

4. Intelligent Variance Classification

Not every difference is a problem. Agentic reconciliation should classify:

  • Configuration difference
  • Timing difference
  • Master data gap
  • Transaction sequencing issue
  • True financial error

And suggest action.

Not just: “Mismatch found.”
But: “Difference due to delayed goods issue posting in target system. Expected to auto-correct in next batch.”

That reduces panic and so many meetings.

Executive-Ready Narrative

Future reconciliation shouldn’t produce:

  • 47 dashboards
  • 16 pivot graphs
  • A nervous data analyst

It should produce:

“Post SAP data migration, revenue and margin remain stable within historical variance bands. Minor inventory timing differences detected in 2 plants with no financial impact. No systemic control breaks identified.”

That’s board ready confidence.

The Big Shift: From Data Movement to Business Assurance

Old model:

Move data. Validate data. Sign off.

Future model:

Simulate business impact. Continuously assure process integrity. Autonomously detect drift. Explain root cause.

Reconciliation becomes less about matching numbers…and more about protecting enterprise behavior.

What Organizations Expect in Future SAP Reconciliations?

In the future, companies won’t ask: “How many records mismatched?” They’ll ask:

  • Are warehouse movements as expected?
  • Did margin integrity hold?
  • Did working capital spike?
  • Did revenue timing shift?
  • Did operational velocity slow down?
  • Did risk exposure increase?
  • Does financial demand and supply are working as expected?

That’s reconciliation redefined in the era of agentic automation!!

entry.title: Master Data Readiness: The Foundation for S/4HANA Transformation and AI Success
entry.content:
Why Data Readiness is No Longer Optional

In the rush to modernise ERP landscapes and adopt AI-driven insights, organisations often overlook a fundamental truth: the success of your S/4HANA transformation and AI initiatives is directly proportional to the quality of your master data. Poor data quality doesn't just slow down migration projects, it can derail AI use cases before they even begin and undermine the business value of your entire digital transformation investment.

As SAP Architects, we've seen the pattern repeatedly: organisations invest millions in S/4HANA implementations and AI platforms, only to discover that inconsistent customer records, duplicate vendor entries, and incomplete material master data render their advanced analytics and AI models ineffective. The promise of intelligent automation and predictive insights remains just that a promise when built on a foundation of fragmented, unreliable data.

  • 85% of AI projects fail to deliver promised value (Gartner)
  • $3.1 trillion annually lost due to poor data quality (IBM)
  • Clear link between data quality and AI failure
  • 40% of business initiatives fail due to data quality issues (MIT Sloan Management Review)

The stakes have never been higher. As organisations race to leverage AI for competitive advantage, master data readiness has shifted from a technical necessity to a strategic imperative.

The Master Data Challenge in S/4HANA Transformations

S/4HANA transformations expose master data issues that may have been hidden or tolerated in legacy ECC environments. The simplified data model, real-time processing capabilities, and integrated analytics of S/4HANA demand a level of data quality and consistency that many organisations simply don't have.

Common Master Data Issues We Encounter:

Data Quality Problems:

  • Inconsistent naming conventions across entities (customers, materials, vendors)
  • Incomplete or missing critical attributes
  • Duplicate records with no clear system of record
  • Inaccurate hierarchies and organisational structures
  • Outdated or obsolete data cluttering the system

Data Governance Gaps:

  • Unclear ownership and accountability for data domains
  • Lack of standardised processes for data creation and maintenance
  • No enforcement of data quality rules
  • Multiple systems maintaining conflicting versions of truth
  • Absence of data stewardship roles and responsibilities

Integration Complexity:

  • Master data scattered across SAP and non-SAP systems
  • No unified view of critical business entities
  • Manual reconciliation processes prone to errors
  • Point-to-point integrations creating data silos
  • Legacy systems unable to provide clean data feeds

These challenges don't disappear when you move to S/4HANA they multiply. And when you layer AI initiatives on top of poor-quality data, you're essentially teaching your algorithms to learn from and perpetuate your data problems.

Why Master Data Quality is the Foundation of AI Success

Here's the reality that many organisations discover too late: AI is only as intelligent as the data it learns from. Machine learning models trained on incomplete, inconsistent, or biased data will produce unreliable predictions. Natural language processing applied to unstructured data with poor quality metadata will fail to deliver meaningful insights.

The Cost of Poor Data in AI:

  • AI models that produce unreliable predictions, eroding business trust
  • Bias amplification when training data contains historical data quality issues
  • Inability to achieve expected ROI on AI investments
  • Manual intervention required to correct AI outputs, defeating automation goals
  • Delayed or abandoned AI initiatives due to data readiness gaps
  • Compliance and ethical risks from biased or incomplete training data

The harsh truth: you cannot AI-transform your way out of a data quality problem. Clean, governed, and unified master data is not a nice-to-have it's the prerequisite for AI success.

 

The Hidden Complexity: Unifying SAP and Non-SAP Data

One of the most underestimated challenges in achieving data readiness is the distributed nature of enterprise data. Critical master data doesn't live in SAP alone it's scattered across:

Common Non-SAP Master Data Sources:

CRM Systems, PLM Platforms, E-commerce Platforms, MDM Solutions, Procurement Systems and homegrown Applications to name a few.

 

The Integration Challenge

For S/4HANA transformations, this distributed landscape creates significant complexity:

  • Multiple versions of truth for the same business entity
  • No single system owns the golden record
  • Manual reconciliation required before migration
  • Risk of migrating conflicting or duplicate data
  • Difficulty tracing data lineage and transformation logic

For AI initiatives, the challenge is even more acute:

  • AI models require comprehensive data across all systems
  • Training data must represent the complete business context
  • Incomplete data leads to biased or unreliable models
  • Real-time AI use cases need synchronised data across systems
  • Data scientists spend most of their time on data preparation instead of model development
  •  

Building the Foundation: A Unified Approach to Master Data

The solution requires a unified master data strategy that brings together SAP and non-SAP data into a single, governed, and trusted source of truth. This foundation serves both your S/4HANA transformation and your AI journey.

Key Components of a Unified Master Data Strategy:

  1. 1. Centralised Master Data Management

Establish a central hub for managing critical business entities:

  • Single authoritative source for customers, products, suppliers, and financial master data
  • Unified data model that spans SAP and non-SAP systems
  • Golden record creation through intelligent matching and merging
  • Real-time synchronisation to downstream systems
  • API-first architecture for AI and analytics consumption
  1. Data Quality and Governance Framework

Implement systematic data quality management:

  • Automated data quality rules and validation checks
  • Duplicate detection and resolution workflows
  • Data standardisation and enrichment processes
  • Workflow-driven governance with clear ownership
  • Continuous monitoring and quality KPIs
  • Data stewardship programs with accountability

Common Pitfalls and How to Avoid Them

Pitfall 1: Underestimating Data Cleansing Effort

The Problem: Organisations allocate less of project time to data cleansing when it realistically requires lots more.

Pitfall 2: Lack of Business Ownership

The Problem: Treating master data as an IT problem rather than a business accountability.

Pitfall 3: Ignoring Non-SAP Systems

The Problem: Focusing solely on SAP data while critical master data resides in other solutions.

Pitfall 4: "We'll Clean It Up After Go-Live"

The Problem: Deferring data quality issues to post-implementation, where they're exponentially harder and more expensive to address.

Pitfall 5: Treating Master Data and AI as Separate Initiatives

The Problem: Running master data improvement and AI development as independent projects with different teams and timelines.

Pitfall 6: Building Point Solutions Instead of Platforms

The Problem: Creating custom data quality tools and integration scripts that become technical debt and can't scale to AI demands.

 

The Business Case: Why Data Readiness Pays

While master data initiatives require investment, the ROI is compelling when you consider both S/4HANA and AI value realisation:

Quantifiable Benefits:

  • 30-40% reduction in S/4HANA migration timeline and risk
  • 50-70% improvement in AI model accuracy and reliability
  • 25-35% reduction in manual data reconciliation efforts
  • 60% faster time-to-deployment for new AI use cases
  • Elimination of duplicate payments and customer records
  • 20-30% reduction in data-related support incidents

Strategic Benefits:

  • Foundation for data-driven and AI-powered decision making
  • Enablement of real-time analytics and predictive insights
  • Improved customer experience through unified views and personalisation
  • Regulatory compliance and audit readiness for AI governance
  • Competitive advantage through faster, more reliable AI innovation
  • Reduced risk of AI bias and ethical issues from poor data

AI-Specific Value:

  • Data scientists spend less time on data preparation, more on model development
  • Higher model accuracy translates to better business outcomes
  • Faster experimentation and iteration on AI use cases
  • Reduced risk of AI project failure due to data quality issues
  • Ability to scale AI across the enterprise with confidence

The question isn't whether you can afford to invest in master data readiness it's whether you can afford not to, given the risks to your S/4HANA transformation and the potential lost value from underperforming AI initiatives.

 

Summary: The Path Forward

Master data readiness is not a one-time project it's a continuous discipline that requires the right strategy, platforms, processes, and people. As S/4HANA transformations and AI initiatives converge, the quality of your master data becomes the single most important factor in determining success or failure.

Key Takeaways:

  1. Data Quality is the AI Foundation - Poor master data quality will undermine AI initiatives regardless of how sophisticated your models are. Garbage in, garbage out applies more than ever.
  2. Unify SAP and Non-SAP Data - AI requires comprehensive data across your entire enterprise. Build a unified master data strategy that spans all systems, not just SAP.
  3. Start Early, Start Strategic - Data readiness must begin before S/4HANA migration and AI deployment, not after. Build the foundation correctly from day one.
  4. Governance is Non-Negotiable - Technology alone won't solve data quality problems. Establish clear ownership, stewardship, and accountability for data quality.
  5. Think Platform, Not Point Solutions - Invest in enterprise master data management platforms that can scale with your S/4HANA and AI ambitions. Solutions like SAP Business Data Cloud can help accelerate your AI journey by providing the unified, governed data foundation AI demands.
  6. Measure What Matters - Establish data quality KPIs and track AI model performance improvements attributable to better data. Make the business case visible and ongoing.
  7. Integration is Key - Build robust integration architecture that can handle real-time AI demands while maintaining data quality and governance across SAP and non-SAP systems.

 

Conclusion

The convergence of S/4HANA transformations and AI adoption creates both unprecedented opportunity and a critical dependency: the quality of your master data. Organisations that recognise this dependency and invest in comprehensive master data readiness will accelerate their transformations, de-risk their migrations, and unlock the full potential of AI-driven insights and automation.

Those that continue to treat master data as an afterthought will find themselves trapped in a cycle of rework, delayed value realisation, and unreliable AI outputs regardless of how much they invest in cutting-edge technology.

The foundation of digital transformation and AI success isn't S/4HANA or machine learning platforms it's the quality, consistency, and accessibility of your master data across your entire enterprise landscape. Whether you leverage SAP Business Data Cloud, another master data management solution, or build your own platform, the imperative remains the same: unified, governed, AI-ready master data is non-negotiable.

For AI initiatives specifically, the data readiness challenge is even more acute. AI models are only as good as the data they learn from. Clean, comprehensive, and well-governed master data across SAP and non-SAP systems isn't just nice to have it's the difference between AI that transforms your business and AI that disappoints.

The question for SAP Architects is simple: are you ready to make master data readiness the strategic priority it needs to be for both your S/4HANA transformation and your AI journey?

The organisations that answer "yes" and act decisively will be the ones leading their industries with AI-powered innovation built on a foundation of trusted data. The others will be left wondering why their expensive AI investments failed to deliver and the answer will be staring them in the face: they skipped the foundation.

 

The window of competitive advantage is closing. Organisations that built their data foundation correctly are already deploying AI at scale improving customer experiences, optimising operations, and making better decisions faster. Those still struggling with basic data quality aren't just behind on AI adoption they're watching the gap widen every quarter.

AI doesn't wait for you to fix your data. Your competitors who prioritised data quality are already leveraging AI to eat your market share.

The choice is stark: invest in master data readiness now and unlock AI's potential or continue treating data quality as "someone else's problem" and watch your AI initiatives fail one by one.

SAP is Here to Help

Recognising that master data readiness is a critical challenge for organisations embarking on S/4HANA and AI journeys, SAP offers dedicated services designed to guide you through this transformation:

SAP Data Readiness Discovery Workshop

SAP Business Data Cloud Discovery Workshop

These workshops provide the expertise, frameworks, and actionable insights to accelerate your master data journey. SAP's experienced Architects work alongside your teams to assess your unique challenges, design practical solutions, and establish the data foundation that both S/4HANA and AI success demand.

You don't have to navigate the complexity of master data readiness alone. SAP is here to help and advice customers on building the data foundation that unlocks the full potential of digital transformation and AI innovation.

Ready to begin your master data readiness journey? Reach out to your SAP account team to learn more about these discovery workshops and how SAP can support your organisation's path to data excellence.

entry.title: Company Code Merge Using DMLT Business Transformation Service
entry.content:
(The Power of One: Streamlining Success through DMLT )

Company Code Merge using DMLT Business Transformation Service : A Comprehensive Guide

Introduction It is noted that company code mergers are an essential component of enterprise transformation, often necessitated by legal compliance, restructuring, or mergers and acquisitions. Financial reporting is simplified through this consolidation, and DMLT Business Transformation services are utilized to streamline the planning, data migration, and support phases.

What is a Company Code Merge? A company code merge is defined as the process by which two or more legal entities are combined into a single unified company code within the system. Financial and master data are transferred into the new entity, while the legacy company codes are subsequently decommissioned,.

When to Use SAP Company Code Merge

  • Legal Mergers and Acquisitions: It is applied when legal entities merge or are acquired, ensuring the system reflects the new organizational structure.
  • Internal Restructuring: It is utilized to combine subsidiaries or divisions, achieving economies of scale and simplified management.
  • Tax or Regulatory Compliance: It is executed to consolidate unneeded company codes while retaining historical data for regulatory requirements.
  • Consolidation of Systems: It is performed when multiple legacy or departmental systems are centralized to reduce IT complexity.
  • Streamlining Financial Reporting: It is implemented to merge smaller legal entities, thereby simplifying the production of consolidated financial statements.

 

Benefits of SAP Company Code Merge

  • Streamlined Financial Reporting: The generation of reports is simplified, as all financial data is consolidated into a single entity.
  • Cost Savings and Efficiency: IT overhead and infrastructure costs are reduced by leveraging a unified operational system.
  • Improved Data Accuracy and Integrity: Data quality is enhanced, and the risk of duplicate records is minimized through centralized master data maintenance.
  • Enhanced Operational Efficiency: Business processes, such as procurement and sales, are standardized and processed more efficiently across the organization.
  • Reduced IT Complexity: System landscapes are simplified, leading to easier maintenance, fewer integration issues, and faster upgrades.
  • Compliance and Governance: Audit processes and regulatory filings are streamlined by utilizing a unified accounting framework.
  • Strategic Agility: Real-time, comprehensive data access is provided to management, enabling faster strategic decisions.

Process of Merging Company Codes

  • Step 1: Pre-Planning and Analysis
    • Scope Definition: The target company code, required data transfers, and project timelines are identified by relevant stakeholders.
    • Impact Analysis: The operational effects on financial statements, IT systems, and compliance are evaluated to formulate mitigation strategies.
    • Gap Analysis: Discrepancies in system configuration and master data between source and target codes are identified for resolution.
    • Project Plan and Roadmap: Detailed tasks, timelines, and team responsibilities are established across all project phases.
    • Change Management: Organizational impacts are addressed through targeted communication and user training preparations.
  • Step 2: Clean-Up and Prepare Data
    • Finalize Go-Live: A cut-off date is established, after which no new transactions are permitted in the source systems.
    • Clear Open Items: Outstanding invoices and unclarified items are settled to ensure accurate balance sheet transfers.
    • Reconcile Intercompany Transactions: Financial balances between the merging entities are settled to prevent double-counting.
    • Archive or Clean-Up Old Data: Historical data is archived or deleted according to retention requirements, often supported by DMLT tools.
    • Data Quality Checks: The completeness and accuracy of all master and transaction data are verified prior to the migration.
  • Step 3: Configure the Target Company Code
    • Align Configuration: Financial settings, such as charts of accounts and currencies, are aligned with the source codes.
    • Replicate Master Data: Master records are created or mapped in the target system to match the source data parameters.
    • Assign Organizational Units: Relevant organizational structures, such as plants or sales areas, are properly assigned to the target code.
    • Transfer Customization: Custom configurations and system settings are migrated and updated to ensure target compatibility.
    • Update Interfaces and Integrations: External system interfaces are redirected to communicate with the new legal entity.
    • Transformations Requirements: Additional technical transformations, such as the merging of controlling areas, are completed if necessary.
  • Step 4: Perform the Data Transformation
    • Migration/Transformation Approach finalization: The transformation approach is decided, potentially including on-the-fly transformations utilizing DMLT services.
    • Extract Data from Source Systems: All relevant financial and master data is extracted as of the established cut-off date.
    • Transform and Validate Data: Data is mapped to the target format and thoroughly validated against the source totals.
    • Load Data into Target System: The transformed data is imported into the target system using batch jobs or specialized DMLT tools.
    • Post Migration Adjustments: Outstanding entries are posted and missing master records are corrected via system adjustments.
  • Step 5: Testing and Validation
    • Reconciliation Testing: Migrated balances and operational transactions are comprehensively reconciled with the source data.
    • Functional Testing: Key business processes are tested to confirm that expected functionalities operate smoothly.
    • System Integration Testing: Data flow and interactions with all integrated external systems are verified.
    • User Acceptance Testing (UAT): Daily operational tasks are executed by end-users to confirm system readiness.
    • Performance Testing: System capacity is evaluated to ensure high transaction volumes can be handled post-merge.
    • Compliance and Audit Checks: The accuracy of financial records and the existence of auditable migration trails are validated.
  • Step 6: Final Cutover and Rollout
    • Freeze the Source Systems: Source systems are locked against new transactions following successful testing phases.
    • Perform the Cutover: Ongoing business operations are redirected entirely to the unified target system.
    • Unfreeze the Target System: Posting periods are opened in the target system to allow for normal operational transactions.
    • Communicate the Change: Internal stakeholders and users are formally informed of the successful system transition.
  • Step 7: Post-Implementation Support
    • Monitoring and Support: Immediate post-merge support is deployed to resolve any arising operational issues.
    • Data Archiving: Archiving of legacy data from the old company codes is conducted using appropriate tools.
    • Decommission the Source Company Codes: Old company codes are fully deactivated in accordance with recommended system procedures.
    • Review and Continuous Improvement: A post-implementation review is conducted to document lessons learned and refine future processes.

SAP S/4HANA and Data Model Changes

  • Universal Journal (ACDOCA): It is required that all historical transactions from legacy codes be loaded into the unified journal structure.
  • Single Business Partner: Customer and vendor records are mapped and consolidated into a unified Business Partner model.
  • Document Splitting: Target configurations are adjusted to ensure financial transactions are split according to new reporting requirements.
  • System Landscape: DMLT services and conversion tools are utilized to ensure a cinsitent and reliable transformation. 
  • Post-Merger Configuration: New consolidation setups and reporting views are established for the merged financial entity.

Key Considerations and Challenges

  • Legal and Regulatory Compliance: Adherence to standard accounting principles and data protection laws must be ensured.
  • Data Integrity and Accuracy: Thorough validation is required to prevent incorrect reporting resulting from migration errors.
  • Master Data Harmonization: Overlapping customer and vendor records must be resolved to create a consistent set of master data.
  • System and Process Impact: Affected business processes and external interfaces must be identified and integrated seamlessly.
  • Downtime and Business Continuity: Operational disruption must be minimized through coordinated cutover strategies and phased rollouts.
  • Organizational Change Management: User adoption is improved by executing comprehensive communication and training plans.
  • Technical Challenges: Complexities regarding custom code, high data volumes, and overall system performance must be addressed.
  • Post-Merger Adjustments: Strategic plans must be established to handle reporting differences and opening balance adjustments.
  • Audit and Compliance: Process transparency must be maintained to ensure all data transfers satisfy external audit requirements.
  • Cost and Resource Management: Project budgets, timelines, and technical resources must be carefully managed to prevent operational delays.

Conclusion

  • Strategic Alignment: The transformation is planned in consultation with key business units to align with organizational goals.
  • Thorough Planning: Scopes and impacts are detailed early in the process to effectively manage expectations.
  • Data Management: Data accuracy and integrity are maintained through the use of DMLT   Solutions.
  • Change Management: Smooth system adoption is achieved by involving stakeholders and addressing concerns early.
  • Use of Tools: High-quality migration outcomes are secured by leveraging specialized DMLT tools.
  • Continuous Improvement: Ongoing system optimization is fostered by conducting detailed post-implementation reviews.

entry.title: Creating a DQM Validation Rule for Business Partner Role Assignment in SAP MDG
entry.content:
Overview

Data quality is critical in Master Data Governance (MDG), especially when dealing with Business Partners. One common data quality issue occurs when Business Partners are created without any Business Partner Role assigned. Since roles determine how a Business Partner participates in business processes (customer, supplier, etc.), missing roles can break downstream applications.

The goal is to create a DQM validation rule in SAP Master Data Governance that prevents the creation or modification of Business Partners without role assignments. The system will show a validation error or warning as shown in the following screenshot:

The validation logic works as follows:

  1. The rule iterates over Business Partner records in table BUT000.
  2. For each Business Partner ID, a table operation checks table BUT100.
  3. If no entry exists in BUT100 for that BP, the validation rule raises an error.

This ensures that every Business Partner has at least one role assigned before it can be saved.

Prerequisites

Before implementing the rule, ensure the following requirements are met:

  • SAP Master Data Governance with Data Quality Management (DQM) enabled
  • User authorization with the SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_STEWARD role or equivalent
  • Access to the SAP Fiori application Validation Rules for Business Partners (App IDs F4084 or F4082A (newer releases)

Rule Configuration

Step 1 — Access the DQM Validation App

  1. Open the SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  2. Launch the Validation Rules for Business Partners app.

This UI/App allows you to manage and create validation rules for Business Partner data

Step 2 — Create a New Validation Rule

Click Create and enter the following information:

Field

Value

Rule ID

CHECK_BP_ROLE_MANDATORY

Rule Name

BP Role Assignment Required

Base Table

BUT000 (Business Partner General Data)

Checked Field

BUT0BK-PARTNER_ROLE (optional but recommended)

The base table must be BUT000, even though the validation checks data stored in another table

Step 3 — Maintain General Information

Fill in the business metadata for the rule:

Description
Ensures every Business Partner has at least one role assigned.

Reason
Business Partners without roles cannot participate in business processes.

Rule Owner
Data Steward responsible for Business Partner data.

Implementation Expert
Technical contact responsible for BRF+ implementation.

Save the rule by clicking Create.

Step 4 — Technical Implementation

Configure Rule Usage

  1. In the Usage section, select Add.
  2. Choose Check in Central Governance.
  3. Click Prepare to generate the BRF+ application.
  4. Select Send for Implementation.

This enables the rule logic to be edited.

Scope Expression Configuration

The Scope Expression defines which Business Partners should be validated.

Steps:

  1. Open the Scope Expression link.
  2. The Simplified BRF+ Workbench appears.
  3. Define the scope.

Example scope:

Partner Category = 2 (Organization)

This means the validation rule will only apply to organizational Business Partners.

You can also configure the rule to apply to all Business Partners.

Condition Expression Implementation

The main validation logic is defined in the Condition Expression.

Steps:

  1. Open the Condition Expression.
  2. Remove the dummy condition.
  3. Create a new expression.

Choose:

Expression Type: Table Operation

Example name: BUT100_CHECK

Table Operation Configuration

Configure the operation as follows:

Parameter

Value

Operation

Has at least

Table

Procedure Call

Procedure

BUT0BK lookup ALL

Condition

Partner Role is not initial

This configuration checks if at least one role exists for the Business Partner.

 

Although the rule loops over BUT000, the lookup ultimately checks role entries in BUT100.

This cross-table validation is the key technical detail of this solution.

Activation and Deployment

Once testing is complete:

  1. Select Send for Testing to change the rule status.
  2. After validation, choose Approve.
  3. Go to the Usage tab.
  4. Click Enable for Check in Central Governance.

The rule is now active and will validate Business Partner data during governance processes.

Testing the Rule

Test the validation logic.

Test Scenarios

Scenario

Expected Result

BP without roles

Validation error

BP with one role

Validation passes

BP with multiple roles

Validation passes

Testing ensures that only valid Business Partner records can be saved.

 

Troubleshooting

Rule Not Triggering

Check the following:

  • All BRF+ expressions are active
  • Rule status is Approved
  • Usage is Enabled

Performance Issues

If validation becomes slow:

  • Restrict the Scope Expression
  • Limit evaluation to specific Business Partner categories
  • Avoid unnecessary table operations

 

Best Regards

Steffen

entry.title: Parallel Class Assignment on Functional Location
entry.content:
1. Issue / Problem Statement

It has been observed that the technical object classification values currently do not meet the expected data quality standards in terms of

  • Completeness
  • Consistency
  • Accuracy
  • Uniqueness

Due to the large volume of legacy data and inconsistencies across multiple sources, the business has not been able to complete the required data cleansing activities within the planned timeline during the S4 HANA transformation.

At the same time, the project team does not want to lose the existing legacy classification data, as it may still provide valuable reference information for historical context, engineering analysis, or future asset validation.

Therefore, there is a need for a mechanism to retain the legacy data without impacting the operational master data quality of the new S4 system.

2. Proposed Solution

Parallel Class Assignment on Functional Location in SAP

To address the above challenge, the proposed solution is to introduce a parallel classification structure on Functional Locations (FLOC) in SAP.

This approach will allow the following:

  • Migration of existing legacy Technical Object Classification values into a separate reference class
  • Preservation of historical data without interfering with the new standardized classification model
  • Future reference and traceability for engineering and maintenance teams

Key Concept

Two classification layers will exist for the same Functional Location:

Classification Type

Purpose

Standard SAP FLOC Class

Clean, validated classification used for operational processes

Reference Class (Parallel Class)

Legacy data stored for historical reference

This ensures:

  • Clean operational data
  • No loss of legacy information
  • Reduced dependency on immediate data cleansing

3. Benefits of the Approach

✔ Preserves legacy technical data
✔ Enables progressive data cleansing over time
✔ Maintains clean operational classification for SAP processes
✔ Supports future engineering analysis and traceability
✔ Avoids project delays caused by data quality remediation

4. Implementation Considerations

  • Create a separate class type for reference data (if required)
  • Define parallel class assignment to functional location.
  • Migrate TOC values into reference characteristics
  • Restrict usage to display/reference only
  • Ensure governance rules to prevent operational usage

5. Project Impact

Area

Impact

Go-Live

Not go-live critical

Scope

Implemented via the scope management process

Business Impact

Minimal operational impact

Data Strategy

Enables controlled transition to clean master data

Functional Location with Standard Class “003” assignment:

Functional Location with “Z03” Class assignment:

SAP Configuration of “Z03” Class for Functional Location:

SAP Configuration of “Z03” Class for Functional Location:

“Z03” Class Organizational Areas Assignment:

Class Type "Z03" Class A11 Organizational Areas G, R assigned:

In Characteristic A0957 Class type “Z03” assignment in Restrictions:

Key Takeaways

In many asset-intensive transformations, legacy technical data rarely meets the required data quality standards at the time of system migration. Rather than losing valuable historical information, a parallel class assignment on functional location (FLOC) in SAP offers a pragmatic solution. This approach enables organizations to preserve legacy technical object classification data as reference while maintaining clean, standardized master data for operational processes. It balances data governance with business practicality, allowing progressive data cleansing without delaying project timelines. A simple yet powerful strategy to protect legacy knowledge while building a future-ready asset data foundation in SAP EAM.

#SAP #SAPAssetManagement #EAM #MasterData #DataGovernance #DigitalTransformation

entry.title: Governance: The Word Everyone Uses and Nobody Agrees On
entry.content:
We're in a strange moment for governance.

The EU AI Act is moving into enforcement. Enterprises are racing to deploy generative AI while their compliance teams scramble to figure out what "responsible use" even means in practice.

And in the middle of all this, everyone keeps using the word "governance" as if we all agree on what it means.

We don't.

If I put your data team, your AI team, and your SAP team in a room and asked them all to define "governance," I'd get at least three different answers. Your data team would talk about quality, lineage, and stewardship. Your AI team would talk about fairness, model risk, and responsible use. Your SAP team would talk about master data workflows, golden records, and change approvals.

They're all right. And they're all incomplete.

Governance isn't a single discipline. It's a spectrum. And organizations that treat it as one fragmented program after another, each with its own framework, committee, and slide deck, keep reinventing the wheel.

Here's the position I want to stake out: the governance problem isn't a capability gap. It's a visibility gap. Most enterprises already have 70% of what they need. They just can't see how the pieces connect.

Data Governance and AI Governance: Two Questions, One Continuum

Data governance answers a foundational question: can we trust our data?

That means defining ownership, measuring quality, controlling access, and tracking how data flows across systems. Most mature organizations have the building blocks: stewards, quality rules, audit trails, access controls.

AI governance picks up where data governance stops, but asks a different question: can we trust what the system does with the data?

This is the shift from managing information to managing decisions. AI governance introduces concerns that data governance was never designed to handle: bias testing, human-in-the-loop design, drift monitoring, and output validation. The accountability surface grows because the impact surface grows.

But here's what most organizations get wrong: they treat these as separate programs. The smarter approach is to recognize that most data governance capabilities are already AI governance capabilities, applied to a different lifecycle stage.

Data quality management builds trust in model performance. Lineage tracking helps surface bias. Access controls define boundaries for ethical use. Auditability enables defensible decision-making.

Same muscles. Different emphasis: inputs versus outputs.

SAP MDG: The Operational Governance Layer Most AI Discussions Miss

This is where the conversation gets practical, and where I want to challenge a blind spot.

SAP Master Data Governance is the operational execution layer. It's where governance policies become enforceable processes, embedded in the workflows your business runs on every day.

When someone wants to create a new vendor record, MDG defines who requests it, who reviews it, what quality checks it passes through, and who approves it before it reaches your production system. After a merger, MDG provides the framework to match, merge, and harmonize records at scale. These aren't administrative niceties. They're governance you can touch.

And here's the connection most people miss: SAP MDG is foundational AI governance that nobody labels as such.

A duplicate vendor record isn't just an ERP nuisance. It propagates into your analytics layer, your spend analysis models, and your AI-driven procurement recommendations. A miscategorized material doesn't just break reporting. It trains your demand forecasting model on flawed assumptions. When MDG enforces a validation rule or rejects a poorly formed record, it is protecting the integrity of the data that AI systems will eventually consume.

This is governance work happening before any model gets involved.

I've seen this disconnect firsthand. A company spends years implementing MDG with robust workflows, quality rules, and stewardship models. Then a separate team launches an AI governance initiative from a blank whiteboard, completely unaware that the operational backbone they need is already running. The disconnect is organizational, not technical. Bridging it is one of the highest-value, lowest-cost improvements most SAP customers can make.

Where SAP Business Data Cloud Closes the Loop

SAP Business Data Cloud takes this further. By exposing SAP's business data as semantically rich, governed, AI-ready data products, it creates the layer where master data quality, business context, and AI consumption connect in one governed structure.

The governance lineage becomes continuous: MDG enforces quality at the source; SAP BDC ensures that what reaches the AI layer is semantically meaningful, access-controlled, and auditable. The questions "is the record clean?" and "is the AI reasoning over trustworthy data?" finally have the same answer, because they share the same governed foundation.

SAP customers building toward enterprise AI don't need to start their governance story from scratch. The architecture is already pointing in the right direction.

The Bottom Line

Governance is elusive because we treat it as a collection of separate problems. Data governance asks whether the data is correct. AI governance asks whether the outcome is acceptable. Master data governance asks whether the record is clean and approved.

These aren't competing questions. They're complementary layers of the same capability: making trustworthy, accountable, defensible decisions at scale.

The organizations that get this right won't have the fanciest frameworks. They'll be the ones that see governance as a single connected spectrum, recognize that most of the pieces are already in place, and have the clarity to wire them together.

Governance isn't a framework problem. It's a visibility problem. And for most SAP customers, the infrastructure to solve it is closer than they think.

entry.title: The Day in the Life of a Data Steward – Meet Susan
entry.content:
When Joe, a quota-driven sales rep, opened his monthly dashboard, he felt a familiar knot in his stomach. His quota attainment looked wrong — revenue numbers didn’t match what he saw in the company’s customer relationship management (CRM) system; some closed orders seemed to vanish, and his commission estimate was off. He needed answers fast. Enter Susan, the Sales Data Steward. 

Susan’s phone pinged with Joe’s message: “Numbers don’t add up — can you help?” She smiled; this was exactly the kind of problem her role was designed to solve. Over coffee, she walked Joe through how she would restore trust in his sales metrics — and how he could rely on a single source of truth going forward. 

Accelerate data projects with data products 

Susan started with the first goal, to build a Sales Performance data product that everyone — sellers, managers, finance — can trust. She connected the primary sources: SAP S/4HANA sales orders, the CRM opportunity records Joe used daily, and the billing system where invoices were issued. SAP Business Data Cloud catalog harvested metadata from each source so Susan could see where definitions diverged. 

She profiled the datasets, spotting familiar issues: duplicate orders, inconsistent currencies, and missing booking dates. To fix this she defined clear business semantics — canonical definitions for “revenue,” “closed order,” and “booking date” — using the unified semantic layer in Business Data Cloud. She set quality rules (standardize currency, reconcile order-to-invoice, remove duplicates) and implemented transformations in SAP Datasphere, a component of Business Data Cloud, to apply fixes and compute KPIs like revenue recognition and quota attainment. 

Before publishing the derived Sales Performance data product, Susan documented every transformation and lineage step in the catalog. When Joe asked how they could trace any KPI back to a source row, she showed him the lineage diagram — from invoice record to summed revenue — so audits and investigations became straightforward instead of detective work. 

A unified, trusted view of your business 

Joe also needed a single view of his accounts. Sometimes an account in CRM had multiple IDs in S/4HANA or marketing systems; duplicates made it hard for him to track customer history. Susan prioritized a customer 360 derived product that linked the consolidated customer master with transactional data (orders, returns, interactions). 

Using SAP Master Data Governance, Susan checked out the data quality dashboards to ensure customer master was up to standards – cleansed and consolidated into golden records. In the Business Data Cloud semantic layer, she defined account hierarchies and segmentation, so Joe’s territory reports and account rollups would always use the same definitions.  In addition, to protect sales contacts and sensitive fields, Susan applied sensitivity labels and masking in the data catalog and enforced access policies for downstream tools. That meant Joe could see the data he needed for selling without exposing PII (Personally Identifiable Information) beyond what his role allowed. 

Ensuring data integrity and trust 

Joe’s commission complaint was the final piece. Compensation calculations and territory mappings had been a source of friction — organizational changes and inconsistent territory definitions across the CRM and human resource (HR) systems were creating mismatches. Susan treated territory and compensation as governed data products: she registered territory and compensation primary products (SAP SuccessFactors for rep assignments, CRM for territories, and the compensation system) and modeled territory and quota semantics in the semantic layer. 

She produced derived products that calculated compensation and applied quota rules, with explicit lineage and timestamped transformation steps. Role-based access and masking protected who could view compensation details. Retention and archival tags supported audit requirements, so if finance or internal auditors asked for the computation trail, Susan could provide it immediately. 

Delivering value and promoting self-service 

Once sales performance, customer 360, and compensation data products were published, Susan put them into the SAP Datasphere Data Marketplace with clear, plain-language descriptions, common queries, KPIs, and her contact details. She added usage examples and steward notes in the catalog, so business users like Joe could discover and use the data without guessing which dataset was trusted. 

Because access was governed and published via delta share, Joe’s analytics tools could connect directly and safely. Susan also continued to monitor the master data, with data quality scores (e.g., completeness, duplicates, currency consistency). When a quality alert fired, she had the ability to execute remediation flows with Master Data Governance. 

Watch a demo of ensuring trusted data products with SAP MDG and SAP BDC

Turn fragmented sources into trusted, accessible data  

A week later Joe refreshed his dashboard. The numbers aligned. Quota attainment matched expectations, commission calculations were transparent, and his account views reflected the single customer 360 view. He could see exactly which invoice contributed to his recognized revenue and who to contact if something looked off — Susan’s catalog entry and contact information were a click away. 

Susan didn’t just fix a dashboard — she changed how the sales organization accessed and trusted data. By registering primary data products, defining business semantics, applying quality rules, and publishing steward-curated data products with documented lineage and governed access, she turned fragmented sources into reliable, reusable data. Joe felt empowered with self-serve analytics while knowing there was stewardship behind the numbers. 

Conclusion 

If you’re starting as a Data Steward, Susan’s playbook maps directly to the operational checklist: prioritize high-value domains, leverage Master Data Governance for trusted master data,  use Business Data Cloud to harvest metadata, register and profile primary products, define semantics in the unified layer, enforce quality and masking, publish derived products to the marketplace, and monitor KPIs and usage. The result is faster time-to-value for business users, fewer shadow-analytics, and auditable, repeatable outcomes for the organization. 

Recommended Resources 

Blog Post: What is a Business Data Fabric? 

Blog Post: What are data products? 

Best Practice Article: The converged data imperative: How to feed AI, govern data, and secure the future 

Blog Post: Master data is a strategic, business-critical asset 

Hands-on: SAP Business Data Cloud basic trial

entry.title: SAP MDG News: AI Use Cases delivered, demoed and presented
entry.content:
Last week, I had the opportunity to present the latest SAP MDG and AI developments to a major client. Since the 2023 release, SAP MDG has unlocked numerous AI use cases, with most integrated directly into Joule and additional embedded AI features available. The live demo showcased all these capabilities in action.

Key AI Use Cases Overview

We're currently seeing three interaction types of Joule with MDG that are transforming how users work with master data:

Use Case 1: Informational

Knowledge at Your Fingertips: With Joule integrated into the SAP Launchpad, business users can ask MDG-related questions without leaving their current workspace. Instead of opening external browsers or applications, users can simply ask questions like "Does MDG support parallel processes for the same object?" Joule accesses the complete SAP Help documentation and SAP Community to deliver high-quality answers instantly.

Use Case 2: Navigational

Smart Data Discovery: Users can pose specific questions about master data within their MDG system. For example, asking "Show me all Business Partners with NWT in their name" displays results directly within the Joule chatbot. Users can then navigate directly from Joule to the appropriate app or action, eliminating the traditional process of opening the correct app, searching for business partners, and then proceeding with tasks.

 

Use Case 3: Transactional

Guided Process Creation: This powerful capability is already available in MDG cloud ready mode. When creating a new Business Partner via Joule, users interact within the chatbot interface, receiving guided assistance with the right questions for BP creation. The system also provides duplicate detection alerts directly within the chat experience

Custom AI Solutions Discussion

Beyond standard use cases, we explored customer-specific implementations using SAP Business AI to enhance master data quality. The concept of an "Attribute Recommendation Agent" could automatically improve data quality through regular evaluation and remediation processes, keeping master data current and accurate through automated quality checks.

Client Feedback Section

The feedback on delivered use cases was very positive! We also discussed exciting possibilities for implementing custom AI use cases with SAP Business AI to further enhance their master data governance processes.

Call to Action

For more detailed insights, check out this comprehensive blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/meet-joule-your-ai-copilot-for-sap-master-data-governance/ba-p/14273811

Meet Joule - Your AI Copilot for SAP Master Data Governance 🤖

Best regards,

Steffen

entry.title: Why master data management is the foundation of AI and digital transformation
entry.content:
Every major digital initiative—ERP migrations, analytics, process automation, and enterprise AI—depends on high‑quality master data: customers, suppliers, products, finance, and other core entities. When those records are inconsistent or duplicated, analytics produce unreliable insights, automations break, and migrations become costly and risky. Master data management (MDM) is the capability set that creates trusted, business‑ready master records and makes them reliably available across the enterprise.

 

Core capabilities that create business value

Focus on capabilities. The following capability groups are what organizations need to deliver trusted master data at scale.

Central governance and lifecycle control: A central governance capability manages creation, validation, approval, and distribution of master records across the landscape. Think of a point of entry fire wall to avoid downstream issues.  This includes adaptable workflows, role‑based experiences, and auditable change processes so business policies are enforced consistently, approvals are traceable, and compliance is demonstrable.

Consolidation and golden‑record creation: Consolidation capabilities ingest master data from multiple sources, standardize and match records, and compute a “best” or golden record. Effective matching and merge processes cut duplicates dramatically and produce a single source of truth for analytics and operations, as well as mergers and acquisitions.

End‑to‑end data quality management: Data quality is a continuous cycle: define quality rules, enforce them at point of entry (single edits, mass loads, or project imports), monitor operational trends, and remediate issues. This closed‑loop process prevents downstream failures and continuously raises the reliability of the data foundation. There is real beauty from being able to go from data quality insight and drill into data quality remediation, especially at a mass scale.

Integration and scalable distribution: MDM must move master data to and from SAP and non‑SAP systems, cloud and on‑prem environments, third‑party enrichers, and business networks. Automated replication, support for common ETL/integration methods, and harmonization across semantic layers ensure master data is usable everywhere and delivered quickly.

Stewardship, monitoring, and operational SLAs: Stewardship tools let roles monitor quality, perform root‑cause analysis, and remediate issues based on business priority. SLA tracking and operational dashboards help teams measure efficiency and tie data quality to business outcomes.

Extensibility plus prebuilt business content: Predefined data models, workflows, roles, and industry content accelerate deployments and reduce risk. An extensibility framework allows teams to adapt governance, add domains, or plug in external services without rebuilding core control logic.

How these capabilities work together (operational flows)

A practical MDM implementation supports typical flows that map to business processes and insights:

  • Central maintenance, validation, approval, replication, and local adaptation: Master records are created/changed centrally in a staging area, validated by business and system checks, approved via governed workflows, and then automatically replicated to downstream systems. Local systems can enrich or adapt replicated data while governance chain remains intact.
  • Consolidation flow: In consolidation scenarios, data is loaded, standardized (optionally enriched via external services), matched using customer‑specific rules, reviewed, and merged into best record(s) before activation and distribution. Business rule frameworks support both standardization and best‑record calculation.
  • Data quality lifecycle: Define quality requirements with subject‑matter experts, enforce rules across entry points, monitor trend and exception reports, and run remediation cycles to fix root causes and refine checks.

Real outcomes you can expect

When capabilities are designed and executed well, they translate directly into measurable business outcomes:

  • Faster migrations and lower project risk: Clean, consolidated master data reduces migration complexity and accelerates moves to SAP S/4HANA or cloud platforms. Prebuilt content and quick‑start templates shorten project cycles and lower cost.
  • More reliable analytics and AI: Trusted master data is the semantic backbone for analytics and generative AI. Models and agents perform better when they are fed consistent, governed inputs; MDM is therefore a prerequisite for scaling enterprise AI.
  • Operational efficiency and cost reduction:  Eliminating manual maintenance, reducing duplicates, and automating replication cuts operational costs and speeds processes—fewer exceptions, faster closes, and better supplier/customer experiences.
  • Stronger compliance and traceability: Centralized governance and auditable change processes make it easier to meet regulatory requirements and demonstrate controls for finance, privacy, and industry standards.

Examples of measurable improvements

  • Faster downstream replication (~98% faster replication in some deployments)
  • Major reduction in duplicates (example: ~98% fewer duplicate records)
  • Near elimination of manual maintenance in multiple systems (there is an example that claims of 100% of manual parallel maintenance removed in certain setups)

Use these kinds of metrics as target benchmarks during business case development, recognizing results vary by starting state and scope.

MDM as part of a broader data strategy

Data fabrics and analytics services are powerful, but they need MDM’s governance and semantic consistency to deliver reliable data products. MDM contributes golden records, governs creation processes, and secures access so data engineering, AI/ML, and analytics can produce trustworthy outcomes. Integrating MDM with an enterprise data layer unlocks end‑to‑end scenarios—consolidated analytics, AI‑driven automation, and adaptive operational agents—that depend on one trusted truth.

Practical accelerators: templates and quick‑start approaches

Time to value matters. Preconfigured systems, quick‑start packages and templates significantly reduce setup time, enable early prototyping, and allow project teams to make design decisions against a working system rather than paper. Combining prebuilt data models, business rules, and workflows with cloud deployment options shortens the route to production.

Choosing the right operating model

MDM supports multiple approaches: central governance with distribution; decentralized ownership with periodic consolidation; or hybrid patterns. The choice should be driven by regulatory needs, business structure, and the desired balance between local agility and enterprise consistency.  And there needs to be a business outcome that is meant to be achieved and an executive sponsor to support it.

Final thought

Master data management is not an IT checklist—it’s a capability play that converts fragmented records into a governed, high‑quality foundation for digital business. Focusing on governance, consolidation, continuous quality, integration at scale, stewardship, and extensibility turns master data into a strategic enabler for migrations, automation, analytics, and enterprise AI.

If you want some further information, check out:

  • Learn more about SAP Master Data Governance: https://www.sap.com/products/data-cloud/master-data-governance.html 
  • Learn more about SAP Business Data Cloud: https://www.sap.com/products/data-cloud.html 
  • Customer Story - Deutsche Börse Group: https://www.sap.com/asset/dynamic/2024/03/00eb05e8-b17e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html
